meta_title: For Students: freelance pitching to Solve can't debug code (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to freelance pitching and overcome can't debug code. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Freelance Pitching to Solve Debugging Issues
content.seo_description: Learn how to use freelance pitching to overcome coding roadblocks. A beginner case study using Antigravity and Claude Code to build your portfolio.
content.answer_block: When you hit a debugging wall, stop spinning your wheels. Instead, pivot to freelance pitching. By offering to solve specific business problems for small clients, you gain real-world constraints that force you to learn faster, use AI tools like Claude Code to bridge your knowledge gaps, and build a professional portfolio.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a terminal full of red text, three hours into a bug that makes no sense. The imposter syndrome is creeping in, and the code feels like a locked fortress. You’re not failing; you’re just approaching the problem from behind a screen instead of in front of a market need.

Freelance pitching is the ultimate shortcut for students. It shifts your focus from 'fixing errors' to 'delivering value.' When you commit to a client project, you gain the accountability needed to push through technical hurdles, utilizing advanced AI tools to accelerate your learning curve while you earn your first stripes in the industry.

steps.title: Define Your Niche Offering
steps.description: Identify a simple problem you can solve for a small business, like a lead capture form or a data scraper, to remove the pressure of 'building the perfect app'.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a technical project manager. Identify 3 high-value, low-complexity SaaS features a local business would pay $500 for. Focus on features that require minimal database complexity.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Research 3 micro-SaaS niches for a student portfolio and outline a feature spec for each.'

steps.title: Drafting the Pitch
steps.description: Focus on the business outcome, not your technical stack. Use the pitch to promise a solution, which forces you to learn the implementation details later.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a high-conversion email pitch for a local business owner. Highlight that I am a developer building a portfolio and offer a fixed-price solution for a specific problem.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Draft a cold outreach email template focused on business value rather than technical jargon.'

steps.title: Architecting with AI Assistance
steps.description: Once the project is accepted, use AI to scaffold the architecture before writing a single line of code, ensuring you don't get stuck in deep logic errors.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a clean, modular file structure for a simple [Project Type]. Include a README and a list of dependencies that are easy to debug.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate the directory structure and initial boilerplate for a project using Next.js and Tailwind.'

steps.title: The Debugging Loop
steps.description: When you hit a bug, stop guessing. Use Claude Code to analyze the stack trace and explain the 'why' behind the fix, turning every error into a lesson.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this error log and explain the root cause. Provide a fix that follows best practices and explains how to prevent this specific error in the future.
steps.claude_code_command: claude debug 'Analyze the current error in the terminal and provide a step-by-step fix.'

steps.title: Deployment and Documentation
steps.description: Finish the project by deploying it to a live environment. A live URL is worth more than ten git repositories in a portfolio.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a concise deployment guide for a beginner to push this code to Vercel, including steps for environment variables and domain linking.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Provide a command-line sequence to deploy the current project to Vercel.'

pro_tips: Always ask the AI to explain the fix in 'ELI5' (Explain Like I'm 5) format to ensure you actually understand the underlying logic.
Use 'Rubber Ducking' with Claude Code; explain the code to the AI line-by-line, and often you will find the bug before it even responds.
Limit your feature set to 3 core requirements; scope creep is the primary cause of unfixable bugs for beginners.
If a bug takes longer than 60 minutes to solve, pivot the feature entirely; your goal is shipping, not perfect engineering.
Save your successful 'Claude Code' transcripts as a personal knowledge base for future projects.
content.next_steps: After completing your first freelance project, update your LinkedIn, publish the live link to your portfolio site, and ask the client for a 30-second video testimonial.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting bugs paralyze your progress. By framing your coding journey through the lens of freelance work, you turn every error into an opportunity for growth. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
